shouldYouSynthesize.detail: Yes — consistently. Synthesis is part of normal progression, and you should be using it regularly rather than waiting until the end of the game.

whatToMake.detail: In general, newer and higher-tier recipes are better SP targets than older ones. If you have more recent recipes unlocked, they are usually the best place to spend materials and time.

methods.name: Item Rebuilding (Unknown Recipes)
methods.detail: One of the most efficient ways to get value from synthesis progress, especially when you already have an item worth improving.

methods.name: Synthesize High-Tier Recipes
methods.detail: Later recipes generally give better returns than repeatedly crafting low-tier items. If your recipe list has advanced, your SP farming should advance with it.

methods.name: Investigate '?' Symbols on the Map
methods.detail: Map discoveries, shrines, and other exploration points can provide useful rewards, so they are worth collecting as you move through the world.

methods.name: Random Quests (Research Team)
methods.detail: Repeatable tasks and side quests are solid supplemental SP sources, particularly when they also give ingredients or other useful rewards.

methods.name: Pioneering Goals & Side Quests
methods.detail: These are one-time rewards, but they add up over a full playthrough and are worth clearing when convenient.

avoid: Do not overfocus on low-tier synthesis if better recipes are already available.
Do not ignore map discoveries while exploring.
Do not assume quality is the only factor that matters for SP efficiency.
Combat EXP nodes are usually lower priority early if your other progression is more important.
